name: n-Pentane
Comment: Experimental data [Sage B.H., 1937] are less accurate than selected ones. Please also seeHossenlopp I.A., 1981.;GT

name: n-Pentane
Comment: Recommended values were obtained from the consistent correlation scheme for alkanes [Scott D.W., 1974,Scott D.W., 1974, 2]. This approach gives a better agreement with experimental data than the statistical thermodynamics calculation [Pitzer K.S., 1944,Pitzer K.S., 1946].;GT

name: n-Hexane
Comment: Recommended values were obtained from the consistent correlation scheme for alkanes [Scott D.W., 1974,Scott D.W., 1974, 2]. This approach gives a better agreement with experimental data than the statistical thermodynamics calculation [Pitzer K.S., 1944,Pitzer K.S., 1946] (see also [Waddington G., 1949]).;GT

name: n-Heptane
Comment: Recommended values were obtained from the consistent correlation scheme for alkanes [Scott D.W., 1974, 2,Scott D.W., 1974]. This approach gives a better agreement with experimental data than the statistical thermodynamics calculation [Pitzer K.S., 1944,Pitzer K.S., 1946].;GT

Comment: Recommended values were obtained from the consistent correlation scheme for alkanes [Scott D.W., 1974,Scott D.W., 1974, 2]. This approach gives a better agreement with experimental data than the statistical thermodynamics calculation [Pitzer K.S., 1944,Pitzer K.S., 1946].;GT

name: n-Nonane
Comment: Recommended values were obtained from the consistent correlation scheme for alkanes [Scott D.W., 1974,Scott D.W., 1974, 2]. This approach gives a good agreement with experimental data available for alkanes. However, large uncertainties could be expected at high temperatures.;GT

name: n-Decane
Comment: Recommended values were obtained from the consistent correlation scheme for alkanes [Scott D.W., 1974,Scott D.W., 1974, 2]. This approach gives a good agreement with experimental data available for alkanes. However, large uncertainties could be expected at high temperatures.;GT

name: Isopentane
Comment: Recommended values were obtained from the consistent correlation scheme for alkanes [Scott D.W., 1974, 2,Scott D.W., 1974]. This approach gives a better agreement with experimental data than the statistical thermodynamics calculations [Pitzer K.S., 1946,Scott D.W., 1951].;GT

name: Neopentane
Final Phase: crystaline, I, order transition, 140 to 142 K

name: Neopentane
Comment: Recommended values were obtained from the consistent correlation scheme for alkanes [Scott D.W., 1974, 2,Scott D.W., 1974]. This approach gives a better agreement with experimental data than the statistical thermodynamics calculation [Pitzer K.S., 1946].;GT

Comment: Recommended values were obtained from the consistent correlation scheme for alkanes [Scott D.W., 1974, 2,Scott D.W., 1974]. This approach gives a good agreement with experimental data available for alkanes. However, large uncertainties could be expected at high temperatures.;GT

Comment: Recommended values were obtained from the consistent correlation scheme for alkanes [Scott D.W., 1974, 2,Scott D.W., 1974]. This approach gives a good agreement with experimental data available for alkanes. However, large uncertainties could be expected at high temperatures.;GT

name: Cyclopentane
Comment: Recommended values are in good agreement with those calculated by [Kilpatrick J.E., 1947,McCullough J.P., 1959] at low temperatures. The discrepancies increase at higher temperatures and amount to 1.8 J/mol*K for S(1500 K) and 2.1 J/mol*K for Cp(1500 K) obtained by [McCullough J.P., 1959]. Calculation [Sundaram S., 1963] seems to be incorrect because discrepancies with these data reach 23 and 7 J/mol*K for S(T) and Cp(T), respectively.;GT

name: Methylcyclopentane
Comment: p=1 bar. Selected values of S(T) and Cp(T) are in close agreement with those calculated by [Scott D.W., 1960] at low temperatures. Discrepancies increase up to 1.8 J/mol*K at 1000-1500 K.;GT

name: Cyclohexane
Comment: There is an appreciable difference (up to 3.0-4.5 J/mol*K) between selected values of S(T) and Cp(T) and earlier statistically calculated values [Brickwedde F.G., 1946,Beckett C.W., 1947,Kilpatrick J.E., 1947,Lippincott E.R., 1966] at high temperatures. It is due to using the most reliable molecular constants in [Dorofeeva O.V., 1986].;GT

name: Methylcyclohexane
Comment: p=1 bar. Recommended S(T) and Cp(T) values differ significantly from earlier statistically calculated values [Beckett C.W., 1947] at high temperatures (6 and 7 J/mol*K, respectively, at 1500 K).;GT

name: Ethylcyclopentane
Comment: p=1 bar. Selected values are in close agreement with those calculated by a method of increments [Kilpatrick J.E., 1947].;GT

name: Ethylcyclohexane
Comment: p=1 bar. There is an appreciable difference, mainly at high temperatures, with values estimated earlier by a method of increments [Beckett C.W., 1947].;GT

name: Ethylcyclohexane
Comment: These heat capacities determined from acoustical measurements are significantly lower than statistically calculated values [Thermodynamics Research Center, 1997] (Cp(298.15 K)=163.9 J/mol*K) and values estimated by a method of increments [Beckett C.W., 1947] (Cp(298.15 K)=158.6 J/mol*K). At the same time, the heat capacities of ethylbenzene determined in this work are only slightly below than calorimetric ones.;GT

name: trans-Decalin
Comment: Recommended values agree with results of statistical calculations [Miyazawa T., 1958,Chang S., 1970] within their uncertainties. These functions are also reproduced in the reference book [Frenkel M., 1994].;GT

name: cis-Decalin
Comment: Recommended values agree with results of statistical calculations [Miyazawa T., 1958,Chang S., 1970] within their uncertainties. These functions are also reproduced in the reference book [Frenkel M., 1994].;GT

name: Benzene
Comment: Please also seeMontgomery J.B., 1942,Pitzer K.S., 1943,Scott D.W., 1947.;GT

name: Toluene
Comment: Recommended values agree better with experimental heat capacities than results of calculation [Chao J., 1984]. All other statistically calculated values [Pitzer K.S., 1943,Taylor W.J., 1946,Scott D.W., 1962] are in close agreement with selected ones, except for high temperatures.;GT

name: Toluene
Comment: Please also seeMontgomery J.B., 1942,Pitzer K.S., 1943,Taylor W.J., 1946.;GT

name: Ethylbenzene
Comment: Recommended values are in good agreement with other statistically calculated data [Miller A., 1978,Taylor W.J., 1946].;GT

name: Ethylbenzene
Comment: Heat capacities determined from acoustical measurements [Colgate S.O., 1990] (124.98, 138.21, 158.84, and 173.88 J/mol*K at 298.15, 323.15, 373.15, and 408.15 K, respectively) are slightly lower than calorimetric ones. Please also seeScott R.B., 1945.;GT

name: o-Xylene
Comment: Discrepancies with other statistically calculated values of S(T) and Cp(T) [Pitzer K.S., 1943,Taylor W.J., 1946,Hastings S.H., 1957,Chao J., 1984] do not exceed 1.5 J/mol*K.;GT

name: m-Xylene
Comment: Discrepancies with other statistically calculated values of S(T) and Cp(T) [Pitzer K.S., 1943,Taylor W.J., 1946,Hastings S.H., 1957,Chao J., 1984] do not exceed 1.5 J/mol*K.;GT

name: p-Xylene
Comment: Among the known statistically calculated values [Pitzer K.S., 1943,Taylor W.J., 1946,Draeger J.A., 1981,Draeger, 1985], the recommended S(T) and Cp(T) values are in best agreement with the experimental data. With the exception of [Draeger J.A., 1981], all calculations agree within 1.2 J/mol*K for S(T) and Cp(T). Discrepancy with Cp(1000 K) calculated by [Draeger J.A., 1981] amounts to 4.7 J/mol*K.;GT

name: Styrene
Comment: p=1 bar. Recommended values agree with other statistically calculated values of S(T) and Cp(T) [Beckett C.W., 1946] within 0.8 and 1.9 J/mol*K, respectively.;GT

name: 1,2,3-Trimethylbenzene
Comment: Discrepancies with other statistically calculated values [Taylor W.J., 1946,Hastings S.H., 1957,Thermodynamics Research Center, 1997] amount up to 5 J/mol*K in S(T) and Cp(T).;GT

name: 1,2,4-Trimethylbenzene
Comment: Discrepancies with other statistically calculated values [Taylor W.J., 1946,Hastings S.H., 1957,Thermodynamics Research Center, 1997] amount up to 5 J/mol*K in S(T) and Cp(T).;GT

name: 1,3,5-Trimethylbenzene
Comment: Discrepancies with other statistically calculated values of S(T) and Cp(T) amount to 1, 2, and 3 J/mol*K for [Thermodynamics Research Center, 1997], [Pitzer K.S., 1943], and [Taylor W.J., 1946], respectively.

name: Tetralin
Comment: Recommended values were calculated statistically mechanically using force field approximation for polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons to estimate the needed vibrational frequencies (see also [Dorofeeva O.V., 1986]). These functions are reproduced in the reference book [Frenkel M., 1994]. Values of S(298.15 K)=368.6 and Cp(298.15 K)=146.6 J/mol*K were calculated using molecular constants estimated by molecular mechanics [Boyd R.H., 1971]. Discrepancies with semiempirical calculation [Szekely, 1955] amount to 14 and 6 J/mol*K for S and Cp at 298.15 K. Cp(298.15 K) calculated by semiempirical calculation [Vvedenskii A.A., 1957] agrees well with value recommended here.;GT

name: Naphthalene
Comment: p=1 bar. Selected values of S(T) and Cp(T) agree with those calculated by statistical thermodynamics method [Dorofeeva O.V., 1986,Dorofeeva O.V., 1988] within 1.2 J/mol*K. Discrepancies with other calculations [Barrow G.M., 1951,McClellan A.L., 1955, 79CHE/KUD,Lielmezs J., 1981] reach 2-3 J/mol*K.;GT

name: Biphenyl
Comment: None of the statistically calculated values of S(T) known from literature [Trevissoi C., 1955,Katon J.E., 1959,Aleman H., 1973,Thermodynamics Research Center, 1997] is in full accord with experimental data (see also discussion in [Chirico R.D., 1989]). Recommended values agree with experimental values within their uncertainties except for temperature range 340-460 K where discrepancies amount to 0.4-0.6 J/mol*K.;GT

name: Fluorene
Comment: Recommended values are also reproduced in the reference book [Frenkel M., 1994]. These statistically calculated S(T) values agree with experimental ones within 1 J/mol*K.;GT

name: Phenanthrene
Comment: These functions are also reproduced in the reference book [Frenkel M., 1994]. Recommended values of S(T) and Cp(T) agree with those calculated by [Kudchadker S.A., 1979] within 1.3 J/mol*K.;GT

name: Anthracene
Comment: S(T) values calculated by [Kudchadker S.A., 1979] are 3.6-4.1 J/mol*K greater than recommended ones. Cp(T) values from two calculations agree within 0.3 J/mol*K. Recommended values are also reproduced in the reference book [Frenkel M., 1994].;GT

name: 1-Pentene
Comment: p=1 bar. Recommended values were calculated from data for lower alkenes by a method of increments. These values are in good agreement with experimental data. The results of the similar estimation [Kilpatrick J.E., 1946] are in poor agreement with experiment.;GT

name: Adamantane
Comment: Selected values were obtained by force field calculation. One can expect the uncertainty in Cp(T) values to be about 10 J/mol*K if compare with the authors' results for bicyclo[2.2.1]heptane.;GT
